sections.summary: Document title (standard name) for dried seeds — nutmeg; not a substantive regulatory provision.
Document identifier and adoption year (CXS 352-2022, adopted in 2022); not a substantive regulatory provision.
Scope: applies to dried nutmeg seeds (as spices) offered for direct consumption, as an ingredient, or for repackaging, and excludes dried seeds and powder intended for industrial processing.

Describes the product (dried nutmeg seed of Myristica fragrans), its post‑harvest operations (stripping, drying, sorting, cracking, grading, grinding), available commercial styles, and optional sizing methods with labelling requirements.
Defines dried nutmeg as the seed of Myristica fragrans that has reached appropriate development, undergone proper post‑harvest operations, and is sold in the styles described in Section 2.2.

sections.section: 2.1.1 Dried nutmeg is the 'seed' of Myristica fragrans Houtt. of the Myristicaceae family (Table 1), having reached appropriate degree of development, i  harvested and treated properly post-harvest, by undergoing operations such as stripping, drying, sorting, cracking, grading, and/or grinding before final packaging, and sold in styles as described in Section 2.2.
sections.summary: Specifies dried nutmeg as the seed of Myristica fragrans Houtt., harvested at maturity and subjected to post‑harvest handling (stripping, drying, sorting, cracking, grading, grinding) prior to packaging and marketed in the styles listed in Section 2.2.

sections.section: 2.1.2 Nutmeg has a variety of shapes from ovoid to broadly ovoid, with a variety of sizes. Nutmeg kernels have a slightly wrinkled-like surface. Inshell nutmeg seeds may rattle due to the seed's shrinkage within the shell in the drying process.
sections.summary: Describes nutmeg physical characteristics: variable ovoid shapes and sizes, slightly wrinkled kernel surface, and that inshell seeds may rattle due to seed shrinkage during drying.

Lists commercial styles (whole inshell, whole shelled, broken seed, ground/powdered) and notes optional sizing for whole nutmegs by count, weight, or diameter with the sizing method required to be labeled.
Style heading: 'Whole inshell' — non‑substantive by itself.
Style heading: 'Whole shelled' — non‑substantive by itself.

sections.section: 2.2.3 Broken seed (obtained from the seed only).
sections.summary: Style heading: 'Broken seed (obtained from the seed only)' — non‑substantive by itself.

sections.section: 2.2.4 Ground/powdered seed (obtained from the seed only).
sections.summary: Style heading: 'Ground/powdered seed (obtained from the seed only)' — non‑substantive by itself.
Optional sizing: whole nutmegs may be sized by count per weight, weight, diameter, or trade practice, and when sized the sizing method must be labeled on the package.

Sets essential composition and quality factors: product composition follows Section 2 and quality factors include sensory requirements and chemical/physical characteristics specified in Annex I, with allowed defects not affecting overall quality and an optional classification when traded as classified.
Composition requirement: the product composition is as described in Section 2.

Quality factors require characteristic odour, flavour and colour free from foreign taints (notably rancidity and mustiness), compliance with Annex I chemical and physical specifications for whole/broken/ground nutmeg, and optional classification criteria when traded as classified.

Sensory requirement: dried nutmeg must have characteristic odour, flavour and colour (allowing geo‑climatic variation) and be free from foreign odours, flavours or colours, especially rancidity and mustiness.

Dried nutmeg shall meet the chemical and physical requirements set out in Annex I (Table 1 chemical characteristics and Table 2 physical characteristics for whole, broken and ground/powdered nutmeg), and permitted defects must not impair the product's quality, keeping quality or presentation in the package.
When dried nutmeg is traded as classified, the chemical and physical characteristics in Annex I apply as the minimum requirements.
Anticaking agents listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) are acceptable for use in the powdered form of foods conforming to this standard.

Products covered by this standard shall comply with maximum levels in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for the Prevention and Reduction of Mycotoxins in Spices (CXC 78-2017) and other relevant Codex texts, and shall comply with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

It is recommended that products be prepared and handled in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969), the Code of Hygienic Practice for Low-Moisture Foods (CXC 75-2015) including Annex III on Spices, and other relevant Codex texts; products should also comply with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Containers shall be as full as practicable without impairing quality and must be consistent with a proper declaration of contents for the product.

Products must be labelled in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre-packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), including the product name (per Section 2.1) and style (per Section 2.2), declaration of country of origin (country/region/year of harvest optional), optional size for whole inshell and shelled styles, and non-retail containers labelled per CXS 346-2021.

Methods of analysis and sampling relevant to this standard are those in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) to check compliance; a sampling plan is not yet provided and is to be developed.

Compliance with this standard shall be checked using the methods of analysis and sampling contained in the Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999) that are relevant to the standard's provisions.
No sampling plan is provided in the standard; the sampling plan is to be developed.
